description: Data-driven result graphics for Lupine Science articles and decks. Rendered as brand-compliant SVGs.

graphics.title: Refrigerant 100-year GWP
graphics.subtitle: IPCC AR6 · mass basis, CO₂ = 1
graphics.note: Lower-GWP refrigerants reduce the non-CO₂ climate forcing from cooling without changing the thermodynamic cycle.

graphics.title: Anthropogenic radiative forcing
graphics.subtitle: Share by agent · 1750–2019
graphics.source: IPCC AR6 WGI Summary for Policymakers, Figure SPM.2
graphics.note: Non-CO₂ agents are ~35% of total anthropogenic forcing; cutting them is the fastest lever for near-term warming.

graphics.title: From predicted crystal to commercial cell
graphics.subtitle: The 0.2% synthesis funnel
graphics.source: Lupine Science analysis; see The 0.2% Synthesis Problem
graphics.note: The bottleneck is not generation; it is validated synthesis under real conditions.

graphics.title: Build-locked theorem inventory
graphics.subtitle: Climate-series formalization
graphics.note: Each jump corresponds to a merged formalization module: climate physics, measured fields, anchor laws.

graphics.title: Participation ratio on the joint error manifold
graphics.subtitle: Classical potentials vs. foundation MLIPs · 15 elements
graphics.source: Lupine Science MLIP Failure Geometry Audit
graphics.note: A ratio near 1 means errors concentrate on a single low-dimensional direction; higher values mean the error is more distributed.

graphics.title: Cross-MLIP error direction alignment
graphics.subtitle: Cosine similarity of dominant error vectors · 15 elements
graphics.source: Lupine Science MLIP Failure Geometry Audit
graphics.note: Values above 0.8 mean different potentials err in the same direction, so a single correction layer can transfer across models.

graphics.title: Error geometry transfers across elements
graphics.subtitle: Classical → MLIP preservation of manifold shape
graphics.source: Lupine Science MLIP Failure Geometry Audit
graphics.note: 14 of 15 elements keep the same low-dimensional error geometry when moving from classical potentials to foundation MLIPs.
